title: Chicago · Forty Years of Crime
subtitle: FBI UCR 1986–2002 + Chicago PD incidents 2003–2026 · 77 community areas · real locations

hook.sub: Chicago Police Department incident data · fully sourced

copy.chapter2Kicker: CHAPTER 2 · THE LAST 5 YEARS · CPD INCIDENT DATA
copy.chapter2Caption: Monthly reports by community area, 2021–2026. Every dot is a real reported incident location (block-level, sampled).
copy.transitionDesc: The chart counted per year. From here the map counts per month across 77 community areas — every dot a real reported location, anonymized to the block.
copy.methodRecentSub: the last five years · by community area, month by month · real locations
copy.methodDotsSub: Every dot is a real reported incident location (block-level). 88.6% of records map to a community area — the rest counted, disclosed, never invented.
copy.methodFootnote: FBI UCR (through 2002) and CPD incident data (2003+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, not one line.
copy.quizQuestion: Which community area is Chicago's safest?
copy.seamExplain: The ruler changes here — not the city. Through 2000 the gray bars are the FBI's yearly tally of Chicago's classic “index” crimes — murder, robbery, assault, burglary, theft. From 2001 the figures are every incident CPD records — a far broader net than the FBI's eight index crimes — so the level steps up because the measure widened, not because crime jumped. Compare the shape within one color, never across the dashed line.
copy.sourceLine: Data: Chicago Police Department · data.cityofchicago.org · modified from original source
copy.coverageText: 88.6% mapped to a community area · pre-2003 citywide
copy.creditsSources: Sources: Chicago Police Department (CLEAR) incident data via data.cityofchicago.org (2001–present) & FBI UCR (1986–2002) via the FBI Crime Data Explorer. Community area boundaries: City of Chicago. Data modified for use from its original source, www.cityofchicago.org, the official website of the City of Chicago; the City makes no claims as to content, accuracy, timeliness, or completeness. Basemap (highways & landmarks): © OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL).

historyNotes.text: 1991: the peak — 320,332 crimes reported in one year, 86,945 of them violent.
The early-'90s peak holds through mid-decade — then the long decline begins.
By 2000, reports are down 34% from the 1991 peak — and the FBI measure ends here.
The fall continues under the city's own fuller count: every one of the 77 community areas declined from 2003 to 2025.
2025 closes at 237,497 — half of 2001. The last five years come next, up close.

annotations.text: Every dot is a real reported incident location — anonymized to the block by the City of Chicago.
August 2023 — the busiest month of the last five years: 24,243 reports citywide.
Austin is still Chicago's busiest community area for Group A crime — but Near North Side is closing the gap.
The long fall has flattened: 240,091 reports in 2022 → 237,497 in 2025.
Edison Park — fewest reported Group A crimes of all 77 areas over these five years.
